exercises.title: How does traceId flow across services?
exercises.instructions:
Payment request travels: Client → API Gateway → Payment Service → Visa Client.
Gateway generates traceId='abc123'. What is the traceId in Payment Service? In Visa response logs?
exercises.expected_solution:
All services share the SAME traceId: 'abc123'.

Flow:
  Client → Gateway: Gateway creates root span, traceId='abc123'
  Gateway → Payment Service: HTTP header X-B3-TraceId: abc123
  Payment Service: reads header, creates child span, same traceId='abc123'
  Payment Service → Visa: HTTP header X-B3-TraceId: abc123
  Visa logs (if traced): traceId='abc123'

Result: Zipkin collects all spans tagged traceId='abc123'
and reconstructs the full call waterfall from one request.

test_cases.expected:
All services share the SAME traceId: 'abc123'.

Flow:
  Client → Gateway: Gateway creates root span, traceId='abc123'
  Gateway → Payment Service: HTTP header X-B3-TraceId: abc123
  Payment Service: reads header, creates child span, same traceId='abc123'
  Payment Service → Visa: HTTP header X-B3-TraceId: abc123
  Visa logs (if traced): traceId='abc123'

Result: Zipkin collects all spans tagged traceId='abc123'
and reconstructs the full call waterfall from one request.

hints: traceId propagates via HTTP headers — same for entire request chain
B3 header name: X-B3-TraceId
Zipkin reconstructs tree from shared traceId

exercises.title: Complete the custom span pattern
exercises.instructions: Fill in the correct custom span creation in Micrometer Tracing.

blanks.hint: withSpan: makes this span the current span
records the exception on the span
ends timing — MUST be in finally

hints: nextSpan(): create child; withSpan(): set as current
start(): begins timing; end(): ends timing (always in finally!)
error(e): marks span as failed in Zipkin (shown in red)

exercises.title: Configure Micrometer Tracing with Zipkin
exercises.instructions:
Write the pom.xml dependencies and application.yml for Micrometer Tracing:
1. micrometer-tracing-bridge-brave
2. zipkin-reporter-brave
3. application.yml: sampling=1.0 for dev
4. docker-compose.yml Zipkin service
exercises.expected_solution:
<!-- pom.xml: -->
<!-- <dependency> -->
<!--   <groupId>io.micrometer</groupId> -->
<!--   <artifactId>micrometer-tracing-bridge-brave</artifactId> -->
<!-- </dependency> -->
<!-- <dependency> -->
<!--   <groupId>io.zipkin.reporter2</groupId> -->
<!--   <artifactId>zipkin-reporter-brave</artifactId> -->
<!-- </dependency> -->

// application.yml:
// management:
//   tracing:
//     sampling.probability: 1.0       # 100% in dev; 0.01-0.1 in prod
//     propagation.type: b3            # Zipkin B3 format
// spring:
//   zipkin:
//     base-url: http://localhost:9411

// docker-compose.yml:
// services:
//   zipkin:
//     image: openzipkin/zipkin
//     ports: ['9411:9411']

// After startup:
// http://localhost:9411 → Zipkin UI
// http://localhost:8080/actuator/metrics/spring.boot.started → confirms tracing active

public class TracingConfig {
    public static void main(String[] a) {
        System.out.println("Zipkin UI: http://localhost:9411");
        System.out.println("Trace data sent after each request");
        System.out.println("sampling.probability=1.0 in dev, 0.1 in prod");
    }
}
hints: bridge-brave: Brave implementation under Micrometer tracing API
sampling.probability: 0.0=no tracing, 1.0=trace everything
Zipkin UI: search by traceId, service name, or time range

exercises.title: Add traceId to log output
exercises.instructions:
Configure logback-spring.xml to include traceId in every log line:
1. Pattern includes %X{traceId} and %X{spanId}
2. Show: what the log output looks like with and without an active trace
3. Why is traceId in logs essential for the 'three pillars' pattern?
exercises.expected_solution:
<!-- Option 1: application.yml -->
<!-- logging.pattern.console: -->
<!--   '%d{HH:mm:ss.SSS} [%-5level] [%X{traceId},%X{spanId}] %logger{20} - %msg%n' -->

<!-- Option 2: logback-spring.xml -->
<!-- <configuration> -->
<!--   <springProperty scope="context" name="appName" source="spring.application.name"/> -->
<!--   <appender name="STDOUT" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender"> -->
<!--     <encoder> -->
<!--       <pattern>%d{HH:mm:ss} [%-5level] [%X{traceId},%X{spanId}] [%X{userId}] %logger{20} - %msg%n</pattern> -->
<!--     </encoder> -->
<!--   </appender> -->
<!-- </configuration> -->

// OUTPUT WITH ACTIVE TRACE:
// 19:05:23 [ERROR] [abc123def456,span001] [] PaymentService - Visa timeout after 10000ms
// 19:05:23 [INFO]  [abc123def456,span001] [] VisaClient - Attempt 1 failed: connection refused

// OUTPUT WITHOUT ACTIVE TRACE (background job, startup):
// 19:05:20 [INFO]  [,] [] ApplicationConfig - Loading configuration
// traceId is empty when no trace is active — expected

// WHY TRACEIDS IN LOGS ARE ESSENTIAL:
// Metrics alert: p99 > 500ms at 19:05:23
// Grafana: sample trace ID from exemplars or known-slow timeframe
// Zipkin: paste traceId → see Visa call took 2.8s
// Logs: grep 'abc123def456' across ALL services → see exact error message
// Three pillars work TOGETHER: metrics → traces → logs → root cause

public class LogPatternExample {
    public static void main(String[] a) {
        System.out.println("With trace:    19:05:23 [ERROR] [abc123,span01] PaymentService - Visa timeout");
        System.out.println("Without trace: 19:05:20 [INFO]  [,] ApplicationConfig - Starting up");
    }
}
hints: %X{traceId}: MDC key set by Micrometer Tracing when span is active
Empty traceId when no span: expected for background jobs
grep by traceId across all service logs: powerful cross-service correlation

exercises.title: Custom span for Visa payment call
exercises.instructions:
Write RazorpayVisaSpanService: custom span around the Visa API call.
1. Span name: 'visa.charge'
2. Tags: gateway, amount, cardType
3. On success: tag responseCode
4. On error: span.error(e)
5. Always: span.end() in finally
hints: withSpan(span.start()): auto-closeable scope; span becomes 'current' inside
span.error(e): adds exception to span AND marks it as failed in Zipkin
span.end() in finally: non-negotiable — missing end() = span never exported

exercises.title: Trace context propagation with RestTemplate
exercises.instructions:
Show how trace context propagates automatically with RestTemplate:
1. RestTemplate with MicrometerObservationClientHttpRequestInterceptor
2. Test: call A → call B → verify B receives X-B3-TraceId header
3. Why WebClient is preferred for reactive trace propagation
hints: ObservationRegistry: the Micrometer abstraction that wires tracing
B3 headers: X-B3-TraceId, X-B3-SpanId, X-B3-Sampled
WebClient: built for reactive; trace propagates through Mono/Flux context

exercises.title: Trace the full Zerodha order flow
exercises.instructions:
Write ZerodhaOrderTraceService with custom spans for 3 operations:
1. validateOrder(): span 'order.validate'
2. checkMargin(): span 'margin.check'
3. submitToNse(): span 'nse.submit'
4. Each span: appropriate tags
5. The whole flow is part of one parent trace
hints: Each sub-method creates a child span of the active span
Thread.sleep simulates real operation time
All 3 spans + parent = one trace in Zipkin

exercises.title: Sampling strategy for production
exercises.instructions:
Write TracingSamplingConfig:
1. 100% sampling in dev, 10% in prod
2. Custom sampler: always trace requests with X-Force-Trace header
3. Never trace: /actuator/** and /health/**
4. Show application.yml and @Bean sampler
hints: Per-profile yml: 1.0 in dev, 0.1 in prod
X-Force-Trace: pattern for QA/support forced tracing
Actuator excluded: Spring Boot auto-config skips actuator endpoints

exercises.title: Add baggage for cross-service user context
exercises.instructions:
Write BaggageService: propagate userId across the entire trace using Micrometer Tracing Baggage:
1. setUserId(userId): set baggage field 'user.id'
2. getUserId(): read baggage field
3. application.yml: configure baggage propagation
4. Show: userId available in all services without explicit passing
hints: BaggageField.create(): creates a named baggage field
remote-fields: transmitted in headers between services
correlation.fields: also put in MDC (shows in log output)

exercises.title: What does the Zipkin waterfall look like?
exercises.instructions:
```
Payment request executes this code (each section takes the shown time):
  POST /api/payments                → starts root span
    validateCard()                 → 5ms
    callVisa()                     → 2,800ms (custom span)
    savePayment()                  → 44ms
  Response returned
Total request time: ~2,849ms
```

Draw the Zipkin waterfall. How wide is each bar relative to the total? Which span is the bottleneck?
exercises.expected_solution:
Zipkin waterfall (total = 2849ms):

Span name            | Duration | % of total | Bar
---------------------|----------|------------|----
POST /api/payments   | 2849ms   | 100%       | [════════════════════════════]
  validateCard       | 5ms      | 0.2%       | []
  callVisa           | 2800ms   | 98.3%      | [═════════════════════════]
  savePayment        | 44ms     | 1.5%       | [═]

Bottleneck: callVisa — 98.3% of total request time!

In Zipkin:
  The callVisa bar is nearly as wide as the root span.
  It will be highlighted in red/orange (slow span).
  Tags: gateway=visa, amount=999.0
  This is the optimization target.

Fix options:
  1. Add circuit breaker timeout (2s max)
  2. Cache card verification results (reduce to 50ms)
  3. Alert when callVisa > 500ms (Zipkin alert or Prometheus metric)

test_cases.expected:
Zipkin waterfall (total = 2849ms):

Span name            | Duration | % of total | Bar
---------------------|----------|------------|----
POST /api/payments   | 2849ms   | 100%       | [════════════════════════════]
  validateCard       | 5ms      | 0.2%       | []
  callVisa           | 2800ms   | 98.3%      | [═════════════════════════]
  savePayment        | 44ms     | 1.5%       | [═]

Bottleneck: callVisa — 98.3% of total request time!

In Zipkin:
  The callVisa bar is nearly as wide as the root span.
  It will be highlighted in red/orange (slow span).
  Tags: gateway=visa, amount=999.0
  This is the optimization target.

Fix options:
  1. Add circuit breaker timeout (2s max)
  2. Cache card verification results (reduce to 50ms)
  3. Alert when callVisa > 500ms (Zipkin alert or Prometheus metric)

hints: Bar width ∝ duration / total duration
98.3% in callVisa: immediate optimization target
Combined with metrics: p99 spike + trace showing callVisa = clear root cause

exercises.title: Full Razorpay payment tracing with Zipkin
exercises.instructions:
Build complete RazorpayPaymentTracingService:
1. Custom spans: 'card.validate', 'visa.charge', 'payment.persist'
2. All spans: appropriate tags + error handling
3. logback pattern with %X{traceId}
4. @SpringBootTest: 2 tests verifying spans are created
hints: spanOp helper: reduces boilerplate for span-wrapped operations
Callable<T>: lambda that returns a value (like Supplier but throws Exception)
Tracer auto-configured: Spring Boot + micrometer-tracing provides a Tracer bean

exercises.title: Trace context in async operations
exercises.instructions:
Trace context doesn't automatically propagate to new threads. Write AsyncTracingService:
1. @Async method: manually propagate trace context
2. CompletableFuture.supplyAsync: wrap with current span context
3. Show: what happens without explicit propagation (orphaned trace)
4. Use Tracer.SpanInScope in the async thread
hints: tracer.currentSpan(): capture before switching threads
nextSpan() in async thread: creates child of captured parent
Without propagation: async work creates disconnected orphan trace

exercises.title: Swiggy order tracing — full 4-service trace
exercises.instructions:
Build SwiggyOrderTracingDemo: trace an order across 4 operations:
1. 'order.validate' (5ms), 'restaurant.check' (15ms), 'payment.charge' (200ms), 'order.confirm' (10ms)
2. Each span: appropriate tags
3. @SpringBootTest: verify all operations complete, no exception
4. Show simulated Zipkin waterfall (as a comment)
hints: Sequential spans: each runs after the previous finishes
payment.charge dominates: 87% of 230ms total
Zipkin comment: helps document expected trace structure

exercises.title: Error tracing — mark span failed, add exception event
exercises.instructions:
Write FailureTracingService:
1. Method that succeeds: span tagged 'result=success'
2. Method that fails: span.error(e), tagged 'result=failure'
3. @SpringBootTest: verify successful span has no error, failed span recorded exception
4. Show how Zipkin displays error spans differently
hints: span.error(exception): adds error annotation, marks span red in Zipkin
Tags visible in Zipkin span detail view
Zipkin filter: can search for error spans specifically

exercises.title: Complete M20 T2 synthesis — trace + metrics + logs correlation
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete Razorpay observability trinity:
1. Counter + Timer (from T20.1)
2. Custom spans (T20.2)
3. traceId in log output
4. Process payment: all three observability pillars active
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): counter, timer, spans all work

All 3 tests pass.
hints: payTimer.record() wraps the entire operation including span creation
Metrics + spans + logs: all active in the same method call
Log statements inside span: traceId in MDC = automatic correlation
